plainEnglish: Headings and form labels must clearly describe the topic or purpose of what they introduce. Vague headings like 'More' or labels like 'Field 1' don't help anyone scan a page or fill out a form. Write descriptive, specific text.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users navigate by headings and rely on labels to fill in forms; vague text like 'More' or 'Field 1' leaves them unable to understand the structure or what to enter.
quickCheck: Scan your headings and form labels in isolation and confirm each clearly describes the section or field it names.
commonMistakes: Vague headings like 'Information', 'More', or 'Section 2' that don't describe the content.
Form labels like 'Field 1' or 'Input' that don't say what to enter.
Reusing identical heading text for sections with different content.

codeExamples.label: Descriptive heading and label
codeExamples.code: Shipping address
